title: Antillanthus cubensis
taxonomy: Antillanthus cubensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Antillanthus, which is part of the tribe Mutisieae. The plant's classification places it within the subfamily Mutisioideae. This classification is based on its morphological characteristics and phylogenetic relationships with other members of the Asteraceae family. Further research may provide additional details on the plant's taxonomic classification.

intro: Antillanthus cubensis, also known as the Cuban silver-leaf, is a plant species belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae) that is native to Cuba and the Cayman Islands. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Antillanthus cubensis include flavonoids, terpenoids, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to alleviate symptoms of colds, sore throats, and joint pain.
